findings.description: The initial migration referenced a nonexistent identity.integration_credentials table.
findings.repair: Use the tenant-composite foreign key to identity.api_credentials and add a regression that rejects the nonexistent table name.

findings.description: The request contract used priority critical while the initial persistence check used urgent.
findings.repair: Align the request, response, TypeScript and PostgreSQL enum to low/medium/high/critical and reject urgent.

findings.description: The initial response schema referenced a nonexistent EvidenceGapPriority definition and omitted request/jurisdiction identity.
findings.repair: Create a closed canonical response with direct priority enum, request_id, jurisdiction and server provenance.

findings.description: Copied ClaimPack scaffolding could have introduced compiler, expiry and content semantics into documentary intake.
findings.repair: Replace the scaffold with dedicated types, mapper, handler, persistence and no compiler dependency.

findings.description: A stored acknowledgement could remain schema-valid while changing limitations or silently promoting source authority.
findings.repair: Require response SHA-256 and exact canonical mapper reconstruction before any transport or aggregate replay.

findings.description: Requester text could be displayed as a RAG finding or used to launder an authority declaration.
findings.repair: Fix request_assertion_status=requester_supplied_unverified and reject imperative authority promotion across subject and reason.

findings.description: Transport idempotency alone did not prove convergence for concurrent distinct keys targeting one aggregate.
findings.repair: Add aggregate identity claim/replay/conflict semantics and prove one-row exact-response convergence in memory and PostgreSQL.

findings.description: Distinct transport keys could compete to create duplicate cases.
findings.resolution: Canonical create hash, transaction advisory lock, unique aggregate identity and sealed initial acknowledgement; concurrent PostgreSQL/HTTP proof passes.

findings.description: JSONB transport replay would not preserve exact response bytes.
findings.resolution: TEXT response body plus SHA-256 and canonical aggregate acknowledgement.
umask 077, explicit chmod 0600, owner/non-symlink checks and artifacts outside repository.

findings.description: One inventory record has acquisition metadata but its ignored bytes are absent from this checkout.
findings.resolution: Do not credit ingestion or durable possession; require authorized object storage, current scan and human review.

findings.description: Source/document/search/evidence/procedure catalog endpoints required by the goal remain missing.
Current credentials do not implement human SaaS authentication/session/provisioning.
No staging/production deployment, observability, load/HA, object recovery or runtime observation exists.
findings.resolution: WS10 platform work and human release approval.

open_external_or_governance_findings.description: Immutable documentary request text lacks an approved purpose, retention, deletion exception and legal-hold policy.
The OS Electoral repository has not run producer/consumer contract, retry and authority-warning tests.
No research assignment/resolution lifecycle, staging, load/HA, observability, backup/restore or deployment is proved.

open_global_findings: Corpus, retrieval quality, procedure cases, authenticated frontend/accessibility and platform operations remain incomplete.
No protected merge or production deployment.
